name: European Brown Ale
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine 3 1/3 lbs malt extract syrup-amber and 2 lbs malt extract powder-light in a large pot with 2 gallons of water.
2. Add 1/3 oz Burton water salts to the mixture.
3. Bring the mixture to a boil over medium heat.
4. Once boiling, add 1 oz Fuggles hops and continue boiling for an additional 15 minutes.
5. After 15 minutes, remove the pot from the heat and add 1 oz Williamette hops. Allow these hops to finish boiling for 5 more minutes.
6. Cool the mixture to room temperature.
7. Transfer the cooled liquid to a fermenting bucket and pitch the ale yeast.
8. Once fermentation is complete, transfer the beer to a bottling bucket.
9. Dissolve 3/4 cup corn sugar in water and add it to the bottling bucket.
10. Bottle the beer and allow it to carbonate at room temperature for about 1 week.
11. Refrigerate the bottles before enjoying your European Brown Ale.
